message:
feat(core): implement streaming multipart parser, UploadFile async API, and temp-file spooling (bd-3ess)

Replace the previous full-body-prebuffer multipart extraction path with
incremental streamed parsing that processes chunks as they arrive from
the request body stream, avoiding buffering the entire payload in memory
before parsing begins.

multipart.rs (~908 lines added):
- MultipartStreamState and parse_incremental() for chunk-at-a-time
  boundary scanning with carry-over buffer management
- UploadFile struct with async read/write/seek/close operations backed
  by either in-memory Vec<u8> or tempfile spooling when payload exceeds
  the configurable spool_threshold (default 1MB)
- MultipartConfig with get_max_total_size() and get_spool_threshold()
  accessors for tunable limits
- Atomic temp-file naming via process-global counter for uniqueness
- MultipartForm::from_parts_with_spool_threshold() to wire spooling
  into the extractor pipeline

extract.rs:
- New parse_multipart_limited() async function that handles Body::Empty,
  Body::Bytes, and Body::Stream variants with per-chunk size enforcement
  and incremental parse_incremental() calls with checkpoint yields
- MultipartForm::from_request() updated to use streaming parse path
  instead of collect_body_limited() + parser.parse()
- Existing test updated for UploadFile bytes() API
- New test: multipart_extract_streaming_body verifying chunked stream
  parsing produces correct fields and file content

Remaining gap: per-part true streaming finalization without buffering
full part content before completion (tracked in bd-3ess).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
